betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Fallout§f

§aNuclear ash scattered by explosions — radioactive but potentially useful for power generation!§f

§bFallout§f is the fission products and neutron-activated materials created by a nuclear explosion. It is pulled into the sky and "falls out" as nuclear ash.

§6Production§f:
§b• Nuclear explosions§f — primary source
§b• Nuclear waste blocks§f — secondary source

§6Behavior§f: Falls like snow, forming shallow layers of granular material. Can be dug with a shovel to yield piles of fallout. Piles can be compressed into blocks.

§6Radioactivity§f: §e30 RAD/s§f in item form

§6SILEX Processing§f (Bismuth laser crystal, 9.0× multiplier):
§b• 90%§f — Tiny Pile of Dust
§b• 2%§f — Cobalt-60 Nugget
§b• 3%§f — Tiny Pile of Strontium-90 Powder
§b• 1%§f — Tiny Pile of Iodine-131 Powder
§b• 3%§f — Tiny Pile of Caesium-137 Powder
§b• 1%§f — Gold-198 Nugget

§6Uses§f:
§b• Radiation-Powered Engine§f — fallout provides fuel for this engine

§eTip§f: Fallout isn't just waste — with SILEX processing, it yields useful isotopes like Sr-90 (for RTGs) and Cs-137. Collect it from your nuclear tests rather than letting it scatter!
